project.name: presidio-hardened-treasury
project.purpose: Audit-grade crypto treasury close for public DAT companies and institutional operators. The system turns hashed observations, policy artifacts, judgments, valuations, journal entries, checkpoints, and external anchors into a disclosure pack an auditor can reproduce byte-for-byte.

layout.Cargo.toml: Rust workspace manifest. The version here is the single source of truth.
layout.crates/treasury-core/: Domain primitives: typed IDs, integer money, bitemporal timestamps, dual control, content hashes.
layout.crates/treasury-evidence/ + crates/treas: The substrate: float-rejecting canonical evidence store and append-only claim-layered ledger.
layout.crates/treasury-chainsource/ + crates/tr: Read-only ingestion core: chain histories, finality, two-source completeness, egress allowlists, key-scope validation.
layout.crates/treasury-reconcile/ + crates/trea: Close gates: internal-transfer classification, dual-control designation, ASU 2023-08 scope assessment.
layout.crates/treasury-lots/ + crates/treasury-: Measurement engine: integer lots, basis-preserving transfers, price snapshots, fair-value reports.
layout.crates/treasury-gaap/ + crates/treasury-: Accounting output: balanced journal entries, dual-control release, idempotent GL posting, mandatory read-back.
layout.crates/treasury-close/ + crates/treasury: Close finalization: checkpoint DAG, external anchoring, content-addressed disclosure pack and auditor manifest.
layout.crates/treasury-conformance/ + crates/tr: Verification layer: one contract suite per live I/O seam and the deterministic golden close.
layout.docs/: Active spec, ADRs, threat model, auditor evidence guide, adversarial reviews.

components.role: IDs, integer money, checked arithmetic, timestamps, actor/tenant/source/venue newtypes, dual-control queue, ContentHash.
Canonical JSON with float/depth rejection, SHA-256 addressing, RFC 6962 Merkle tree heads, file-backed evidence store.
Bitemporal append-only event ledger with per-tenant hash chains, claim-layer provenance, supersession, durable replay verification.
Content-addressed approval-signed policy artifacts, activation timelines, valuation key over lots, price snapshot, and policy hash.
Pure-domain chain source abstraction, finality policies, reproducibility gate, two-source history reconciliation, L1 observation drafting.
Read-only egress allowlist and venue key-scope validator. Deny by default; unknown or trading scopes fail closed.
Tiered internal-transfer matcher, close blockers, auto-net L2 booking, dual-control confirmation queue, non-purchase designation.
ASU 2023-08 six-criterion scope gate. Confirmed in-scope assets proceed; unassessed, undetermined, or out-of-scope assets block.
Integer lot book, fee decomposition, specific/FIFO relief, basis-preserving internal transfers, lots_hash input to valuation.
Integer-exact valuation as a pure function of lots, price snapshot, and principal-market policy hash. Missing prices fail closed.
GAAP L4 module. Balanced journal entries are constructed by type; ASU 2023-08 fair-value marks route to net income.
Vendor-neutral posting protocol with content-derived idempotency key, dual-control release, Unknown state, and read-back verification.
GL adapter contract and fixture GL. Read-back is a required trait method, so a post-only adapter cannot exist.
Closed-period checkpoint DAG. Supersession is append-only and requires reason plus materiality memo.
Coverage-monotonic anchor log, Merkle aggregation, submission lifecycle, depth gating, overdue-liveness.
Roll-forward rows that must roll, two-way valuation tie-out, content-addressed disclosure pack, evidence manifest.
Reusable contract suites for ChainSource, ChainAnchorSubmitter, and GlAdapter; fixtures pass today, live shims must pass before trust.
Golden close across every stage. Runs the quarter twice and asserts identical artifact hashes.

flows.name: Quarterly Close Pipeline
flows.summary: The end-to-end path exercised by treasury-e2e: fixture-backed observations enter the ledger, the close gates classify and measure them, GL output is verified, the period is sealed, and the disclosure pack commits to every referenced hash.
diagram: EvidenceStore -> L1 Ledger observations -> Reconciliation -> Scope gate
Lots + PriceSnapshot + Policy -> Valuation -> GAAP entries -> Posting protocol -> Fixture GL read-back
Checkpoint DAG --------------> Anchor receipt -> Disclosure pack -> Auditor manifest

steps.label: Register policies
steps.detail: Principal-market and designation policies are content-addressed and activated on the tenant timeline.

steps.label: Book L1 observations
steps.detail: Purchase, withdrawal, deposit, and staking reward payloads are evidence-hashed before entering the append-only ledger.

steps.label: Reconcile internal transfer
steps.detail: Withdrawal/deposit pair auto-nets as a tier-0 internal transfer and books an L2 derived fact.

steps.label: Resolve human judgments
steps.detail: Staking reward designation and BTC scope assessment both require distinct preparer/approver actors and book as L3 judgments.

steps.label: Build lots and value book
steps.detail: Acquisition, internal transfer, reward lot, and price snapshot produce a deterministic valuation hash.

steps.label: Emit accounting outputs
steps.detail: Acquisition and remeasurement entries are structurally balanced and book as L4 policy outputs.

steps.label: Post and read back
steps.detail: The posting batch is released under dual control, submitted by idempotency key, then verified by read-back fingerprint.

steps.label: Seal and anchor
steps.detail: The checkpoint commits to the ledger state root; the anchor receipt commits to the evidence-store tree head.

steps.label: Publish disclosure pack
steps.detail: Roll-forward rows must tie to valuation, and the manifest closes over checkpoint, valuation, policy, anchor, and entry hashes.

steps.label: Assert determinism
steps.detail: The full close runs twice and every final artifact hash matches byte-for-byte.

notes: The current golden close uses deterministic fixtures, not live blockchain or GL endpoints.
The same pure-domain assertions become live integration gates through treasury-conformance.

flows.name: Chain Evidence Completeness
flows.summary: Chain ingestion deliberately stops at a pure trait seam. Two independent sources produce settled address histories; the hashes must match before an L1 observation can be booked.
diagram: BTC: Core + electrs/Fulcrum      ETH: reth + Erigon
FinalityPolicy -> settled prefix -> history hash comparison

steps.label: Normalize history
steps.detail: Movements are chain-agnostic, integer-only, canonically ordered, and content-addressed.

steps.label: Apply finality
steps.detail: Bitcoin uses confirmation depth; Ethereum uses an externally supplied finalized height.

steps.label: Re-query source
steps.detail: The same source must return the same settled-history hash for the same query.

steps.label: Compare independent sources
steps.detail: Two settled histories must hash-match. A divergence names both sources and blocks close.

steps.label: Book agreed history
steps.detail: Only an agreed reconciliation maps to an L1 ledger observation.

errors: WrongChain — source reports a chain other than the configured chain
NotReproducible — re-query changes the settled-history hash
Diverged — independent histories disagree; close blocks, nothing is booked
Unsettled movement — excluded from comparison until finality policy admits it

flows.name: Claim-Layer Ledger
flows.summary: Every booked fact is classified by claim layer, and the layer dictates admissible provenance. Corrections supersede; they never mutate the old fact.
diagram: L2 derived fact -> input event ids + code/config hash
L3 judgment     -> preparer + distinct approver + policy hash
L4 output       -> policy hash + input closure
append-only per-tenant hash chain + bitemporal as_of queries

steps.label: Draft event
steps.detail: Tenant, layer, event time, supersession edge, provenance, and canonical payload define the proposed fact.

steps.label: Validate provenance
steps.detail: The ledger rejects layer/provenance mismatches, missing approvers, cross-layer supersession, and float payloads.

steps.label: Seal identity
steps.detail: The event id commits to the canonical envelope and previous per-tenant event id.

steps.label: Query bitemporally
steps.detail: Knowledge-time queries answer what the books said at filing time versus what they say after correction.

steps.label: Verify chain
steps.detail: Mutation, insertion, or deletion after append breaks the recomputed hash chain.

flows.name: Internal Transfer Reconciliation
flows.summary: The matcher is biased toward false negatives: an ambiguous leg blocks close instead of being mis-booked as a disposal and acquisition.
diagram: +-- Tier 0: same tx hash -----------------> auto-net L2
+-- Tier 1: amount-fee + time + address --> auto-net below materiality, queue above
+-- Tier 2: ambiguity or weak signal -----> dual-control queue

steps.label: Sort deterministically
steps.detail: Legs are processed by event time and leg id so reordering inputs cannot change results.

steps.label: Classify by corroboration tier
steps.detail: Tiers are discrete classes, not numeric confidence scores.

steps.label: Auto-net only where defensible
steps.detail: Tier-0 and below-materiality tier-1 matches can book L2 derived facts.

steps.label: Queue material judgments
steps.detail: Queued matches require a preparer and a distinct approver before booking.

steps.label: Designate non-purchase inflows
steps.detail: Staking rewards and similar non-purchase acquisitions book as L3 judgments only after dual control.

errors: Ambiguity — no auto-net; queue or block close
Missing materiality threshold — fail closed to queue
Preparer self-confirmation — rejected by dual control
Unmatched leg — blocks period close until classified

flows.name: Valuation to GL Posting
flows.summary: Measurement and posting stay deterministic: valuation is keyed by lots, price snapshot, and policy hash; GL release and verification are state-machine transitions.
diagram: dual-control release -> submit -> Unknown/Posted -> read-back verify

steps.label: Measure positions
steps.detail: All arithmetic is integer minor units and checked i128; prices are rational integer pairs.

steps.label: Fail on missing price
steps.detail: No engine default or stale fallback can silently value an asset.

steps.label: Build balanced entries
steps.detail: Debits and credits must balance in one currency or the value is unconstructible.

steps.label: Register batch
steps.detail: The batch hash commits to tenant, target GL, period, and entry hashes; it is the retry idempotency key.

steps.label: Release under dual control
steps.detail: Posting cannot begin until an approver distinct from the preparer releases the batch.

steps.label: Verify read-back
steps.detail: The GL read-back fingerprint must equal the batch fingerprint both directions; mismatches name missing and unexpected entries.

errors: MissingPrice — valuation refuses to produce a report
CurrencyMismatch — basis and snapshot currencies differ
UnbalancedEntry — journal entry cannot be constructed
ReadbackMismatch — posted GL entries do not match the batch

flows.name: Evidence, Anchoring, and Auditor Reproduction
flows.summary: Hash chaining alone is not enough because the operator is inside the threat model. The evidence tree head is anchored outside the trust boundary, and the disclosure pack tells an auditor exactly what to fetch and recompute.
diagram: evidence blobs -> canonical bytes -> SHA-256 content addresses
RFC 6962 tree head -> Merkle aggregation -> ChainAnchorSubmitter
AnchorLog verifies prefix coverage       confirmed receipt

steps.label: Canonicalize inputs
steps.detail: JSON payloads are sorted, compact, depth-capped, and reject floats.

steps.label: Store by hash
steps.detail: Durable stores verify every blob against its recorded hash on open.

steps.label: Commit tree head
steps.detail: The evidence store's RFC 6962 head summarizes the append-only evidence prefix.

steps.label: Aggregate and submit
steps.detail: Multiple heads can be aggregated into one anchor target while preserving per-head inclusion proofs.

steps.label: Gate on confirmations
steps.detail: Anchored status requires sufficient depth; submitted-but-unconfirmed anchors can become overdue instead of silent gaps.

steps.label: Reproduce from pack hash
steps.detail: The manifest is the sorted closure of every artifact hash required to reproduce the close.

errors: FloatRejected — non-deterministic numeric input cannot become evidence
CoverageRegression — anchor entry count cannot move backward
DepthBelowThreshold — receipt is not final enough to trust
OverdueAnchor — submission liveness gap is visible
TieBreak — disclosure rows do not tie to valuation

flows.name: Live I/O Conformance Boundary
flows.summary: Production integrations are deliberately outside the pure-domain crates. Before any real endpoint is trusted, it must pass the same conformance contract that fixtures pass today.

steps.label: Implement trait shim
steps.detail: The live client lives out-of-core and reports normalized domain values into the pure system.

steps.label: Run fixture contract
steps.detail: Fixture self-tests prove the harness and in-memory implementations satisfy the seam invariants.

steps.label: Run live endpoint contract
steps.detail: A real endpoint must satisfy identity, reproducibility, liveness, idempotency, and read-back obligations before it enters the evidence path.

steps.label: Keep residual honest
steps.detail: Live-only failure modes are documented per seam, not claimed away by fixture tests.

modes.name: ChainSource
modes.detail: Verifies non-empty source identity, correct chain, byte-for-byte reproducibility, settled-prefix stability, and two-source agreement.

modes.name: ChainAnchorSubmitter
modes.detail: Verifies idempotent submission, receipt completeness, confirmation liveness, and failure surfacing.

modes.name: GlAdapter
modes.detail: Verifies submit/read-back behavior, retry safety after lost acknowledgments, and mismatch detection.

notes: Fixture tests need no live service and can run with Docker networking disabled.
Future live blockchain integration jobs should use regtest or public testnet fixtures until a production partner explicitly provides real holdings and audit scope.
The pure close remains deterministic even when concrete I/O shims change.

flows.name: Fail-Closed Close Blockers
flows.summary: The negative close tests document what the system refuses to guess about. In every case the safer behavior is to block close or reject construction.

guards.blocks: No L1 observation is booked; completeness gate blocks close.

guards.condition: Source changes settled history on re-query
guards.blocks: Reproducibility gate rejects the source before booking.

guards.condition: Asset is unassessed, undetermined, or out of ASU 2023-08 scope
guards.blocks: Valuation does not run for that asset.

guards.condition: Price snapshot lacks a held asset
GL read-back differs from posted batch
guards.blocks: Posting remains unverified and names the mismatch.

guards.condition: Anchor submits but does not confirm in budget
guards.blocks: Overdue state is visible; coverage gap is not silent.
